question: What is the Payment & Performance Bond Article #?
What are the Payment & Performance Bond Article Terms & Conditions?
value: If Contractor is not required to maintain Subcontractor Default Insurance, or for any Subcontractor not covered by Contractor’s Subcontractor Default Insurance policy, Contractor shall require that each of its Subcontractors with Subcontracts in excess of $200,000, prior to commencing its respective portion of the Work, obtain a performance bond and a payment bond, in form and substance satisfactory to Owner, in the full amount of the Subcontractor’s contract price, from a surety company authorized to do business in the state in which the Project is located holding a valid certificate of authority issued by the Secretary of the U.S. Treasury as an acceptable surety on federal bonds and which is otherwise satisfactory to Owner. All performance bonds and payment bonds shall name Contractor as obligee and shall name Owner as additional obligee. All such payment bonds and performance bonds shall be delivered by Contractor to Owner for Owner’s approval before each Subcontractor commences its respective portion of the Work.
raw_answer: Contractor shall require that each of its Subcontractors with Subcontracts in excess of $200,000, prior to commencing its respective portion of the Work, obtain a performance bond and a payment bond, in form and substance satisfactory to Owner, in the full amount of the Subcontractor’s contract price.

question: What is the Contract Documents Definition & Incorporation Article #?
raw_answer: THE CONTRACT DOCUMENTS

question: What are the Contract Documents Definition & Incorporation Definitions, Terms, & Conditions?
value: The Contract Documents consist of the Task Order, the Master Terms and Conditions of the Contract for Construction dated March 29, 2021, executed by Owner and Contractor, and all other documents identified as Contract Documents in the Task Order or in the Master Terms and Conditions. The Contractor acknowledges that it is bound by the Master Terms and Conditions as if they had been executed on the date hereof and set forth in full herein. All terms not defined in the Task Order shall have the respective meanings given to such terms in the other Contract Documents. The Contract Documents and the Task Order shall not be binding upon the Owner for a project for which the original Guaranteed Maximum Price exceeds Five Million Dollars ($5,000,000.00).
raw_answer: The Contract Documents consist of the Task Order, the Master Terms and Conditions of the Contract for Construction dated March 29, 2021, executed by Owner and Contractor, and all other documents identified as Contract Documents in the Task Order or in the Master Terms and Conditions.

question: What is the IMC Design Document Review obligations Article #?
raw_answer: Article 7

question: What are the Terms & Conditions / IMC's obligations for Design Document Review?
value: Contractor shall comply with all of the requirements applicable to Contractor, or assigned to Contractor by Owner, as provided in the Building Information Modeling Requirements (“BIM Requirements”) attached hereto as Exhibit L. Owner and Contractor acknowledge and agree that neither the Building Information Model nor any Model Element (as said terms are defined in the BIM Requirements) is a Contract Document and does not alter or supersede in any respect the Drawings and Specifications or any other design document prepared by Architect and its consultants. Without limitation of the foregoing, Contractor shall throughout the performance of the Work maintain accurate and current electronic “as built” records of all material changes in the Work made by Contractor or its Subcontractors in the field from what is shown on the Construction Documents, as the same may be modified during the course of the Work in accordance with the terms of the Contract Documents, and 3D shop drawings, construction coordination models and all other documentation required by Exhibit L hereto so that Architect may employ such records to prepare record drawings in BIM format. As a condition precedent to Owner’s obligation to make payment with respect to any Application for Payment, Contractor shall demonstrate to Owner and Architect that Contractor’s records as aforesaid are accurate and correct, and upon request from time to time by Owner or Architect, shall deliver to Architect complete copies of such records, active files of which shall be maintained at the Project site. Nothing contained herein shall authorize any changes in the Work except as authorized by a duly executed Change Order.
raw_answer: Contractor shall comply with all of the requirements applicable to Contractor, or assigned to Contractor by Owner, as provided in the Building Information Modeling Requirements (“BIM Requirements”) attached hereto as Exhibit L.

question: What is the Submittal Alternates, Substitutions, & Contract Document Deviations Article #?
raw_answer: Article 7

question: What are the Submittal Alternates, Substitutions, & Contract Document Deviations Terms & Conditions?
value: Submittal Alternates, Substitutions, & Contract Document Deviations Terms & Conditions are outlined in various sections of the contract documents. All allowances and alternates included in the Guaranteed Maximum Price are set forth in Exhibit B. Exhibit B also designates those alternates accepted by the Owner and those which may be accepted in the future, along with the corresponding adjustments to the Guaranteed Maximum Price. The Contractor must comply with all Building Information Modeling (BIM) requirements as provided in Exhibit L, and maintain accurate and current electronic 'as built' records of all material changes in the Work. Any changes in the Work must be authorized by a duly executed Change Order. The Contractor is also required to maintain a policy of Subcontractor Default Insurance, which must meet specific requirements, including coverage limits and endorsements for the benefit of the Owner. In the event of cancellation or reduction in coverage, the Contractor must obtain completion bonds and labor and material payment bonds for all Subcontractors whose work is not fully completed.
raw_answer: Submittal Alternates, Substitutions, & Contract Document Deviations Terms & Conditions are outlined in various sections of the contract documents, including Exhibit B and Exhibit L, and involve maintaining accurate records, complying with BIM requirements, and ensuring proper insurance coverage.

question: Must the GMP Buyout Savings be Shown on Monthly Schedule of Values?
What are the GMP Buyout Savings Definition & Terms & Conditions?
value: GMP Buyout Savings refers to the difference between the Contract Sum and the Guaranteed Maximum Price (GMP). If the Contract Sum is less than the GMP, the entire amount of the difference benefits the Owner. Adjustments to the Contract Sum due to changes in the Work are determined according to Article 7 of the Master Terms and Conditions. Cash discounts, trade discounts, rebates, refunds, and amounts from sales of surplus materials and equipment accrue to the Owner. Insurance proceeds received by the Contractor also accrue to the Owner. These amounts are credited to the Owner as a deduction from the Cost of the Work.
raw_answer: GMP Buyout Savings refers to the difference between the Contract Sum and the Guaranteed Maximum Price (GMP). If the Contract Sum is less than the GMP, the entire amount of the difference benefits the Owner. Adjustments to the Contract Sum due to changes in the Work are determined according to Article 7 of the Master Terms and Conditions. Cash discounts, trade discounts, rebates, refunds, and amounts from sales of surplus materials and equipment accrue to the Owner. Insurance proceeds received by the Contractor also accrue to the Owner. These amounts are credited to the Owner as a deduction from the Cost of the Work.

question: What is the definition for the Cost of the Work?
value: The term 'Cost of the Work' shall mean costs necessarily incurred by the Contractor, directly or in connection with Subcontracts, in good faith and in the proper performance of the Work. Such costs shall be at rates not higher than the standard paid at the location of the Project except with prior consent of the Owner. All costs in the costs of the Work shall be reasonable and customary. The Cost of the Work shall include only the following items: wages of construction workers, wages or salaries of supervisory and administrative personnel, costs for taxes, insurance, contributions, assessments and benefits, payments to Subcontractors, costs of materials and equipment, rental charges for temporary facilities, machinery, equipment, and hand tools, costs of removal of debris, costs of document reproductions and postage, travel expenses, insurance and bond premiums, sales and use taxes, fees and assessments for permits, licenses and inspections, fees of testing laboratories, deposits lost for causes other than the Contractor's negligence, other costs approved in advance in writing by the Owner, and costs due to emergencies.
raw_answer: The term 'Cost of the Work' shall mean costs necessarily incurred by the Contractor, directly or in connection with Subcontracts, in good faith and in the proper performance of the Work.

question: What is the Payment Terms for Offsite Stored Material Article #?
What are the Terms and Conditions for Payment of Offsite Stored Material?
value: Payments for offsite stored materials are subject to the following conditions: Costs, including transportation and storage, of materials and equipment incorporated or delivered and properly stored on-site to be incorporated in the completed construction are included. Costs of materials in excess of those actually installed but required to provide reasonable allowance for waste and spoilage are also included. Unused excess materials shall be properly stored during the performance of the Work and shall be delivered to the Owner. Rental charges for temporary facilities, machinery, equipment, and hand tools not customarily owned by construction workers that are provided by the Contractor at the Project site are included, subject to the Owner's prior written approval. Rental charges for machinery and equipment furnished by Contractor from its own stock shall not exceed the fair rental value for the same in the region where the Project is located, and the total rental of any piece of such machinery or equipment shall not exceed its fair market value.
raw_answer: Payments for offsite stored materials are subject to conditions including transportation and storage costs, reasonable allowance for waste and spoilage, proper storage of unused materials, and rental charges for temporary facilities and equipment with prior written approval from the Owner.

question: What are the Terms & Conditions for IMC's Stop Work Ability for late Owner payment?
value: Contractor shall have the right to stop work if the Owner fails to make payment within the time frame specified in the Contract Documents. The Contractor must provide written notice to the Owner of the intent to stop work, and if the payment is not received within seven (7) days after the notice, the Contractor may stop work until payment is made. The Contractor shall be entitled to an extension of time and reimbursement for costs incurred due to the stoppage.
raw_answer: Contractor shall have the right to stop work if the Owner fails to make payment within the time frame specified in the Contract Documents.

question: What is the Final Payment Article #?
raw_answer: Article 13

question: What are the Final Payment Terms & Conditions?
value: The final payment terms and conditions are as follows: The Contract Sum is the lesser of the Cost of the Work plus the Contractor’s Fee or the Guaranteed Maximum Price. The Contractor’s Fee is 3% of the Cost of the Work, subject to the Guaranteed Maximum Price limitation. The Guaranteed Maximum Price is $2,822,335. Adjustments to the Contract Sum on account of changes in the Work shall be determined in accordance with Article 7 of the Master Terms and Conditions. All payments made by the Owner pursuant to the Task Order shall be credited against the Guaranteed Maximum Price. The Contractor shall maintain accurate and current electronic 'as built' records of all material changes in the Work. As a condition precedent to Owner’s obligation to make payment with respect to any Application for Payment, Contractor shall demonstrate to Owner and Architect that Contractor’s records are accurate and correct. The Cost of the Work shall not include any bonds obtained for Subcontractors covered by Contractor’s Subcontractor Default Insurance policy. Contractor shall comply with all BIM Requirements. The Contractor shall not remove machinery or equipment from the Project site without the Owner’s prior written approval. Costs of complying with the BIM Requirements, removal of debris, document reproductions, postage, parcel delivery charges, travel expenses, insurance, bond premiums, sales and use taxes, fees for permits, licenses, inspections, testing laboratories, and other costs incurred in the performance of the Work if approved in advance in writing by the Owner are included. Costs due to emergencies incurred in taking action to prevent threatened damage, injury, or loss are also included. The Contractor shall not duplicate payments for any items comprising the Cost of the Work. The Cost of the Work shall not include salaries and other compensation of the Contractor's personnel, expenses of the Contractor's principal office, overhead and general expenses, capital expenses, rental costs of machinery and equipment, and costs due to the fault or negligence of the Contractor or Subcontractors.
raw_answer: The final payment terms and conditions include the Contract Sum being the lesser of the Cost of the Work plus the Contractor’s Fee or the Guaranteed Maximum Price, with the Contractor’s Fee being 3% of the Cost of the Work and the Guaranteed Maximum Price being $2,822,335. Adjustments to the Contract Sum are determined by Article 7 of the Master Terms and Conditions. Payments made by the Owner are credited against the Guaranteed Maximum Price. The Contractor must maintain accurate 'as built' records and comply with BIM Requirements. Costs include those for complying with BIM Requirements, removal of debris, document reproductions, postage, travel expenses, insurance, bond premiums, sales and use taxes, fees for permits, licenses, inspections, testing laboratories, and other approved costs. Costs due to emergencies are also included. The Cost of the Work excludes salaries and other compensation of the Contractor's personnel, expenses of the Contractor's principal office, overhead and general expenses, capital expenses, rental costs of machinery and equipment, and costs due to the fault or negligence of the Contractor or Subcontractors.
